sources.title: State of Nevada Board of Wildlife Commissioners — Fishing Seasons CR 25-16 (Jan 1, 2026 – Dec 31, 2027)
sources.quotedText: Washoe County Community Fishing Ponds: Battle Born Pond, Davis Creek Pond, Idlewild Ponds, Marilyn's Pond, Paradise Ponds, Rancho San Rafael Pond, Sparks Marina Pond, Verdi Pond, Virginia Lake and Wilson Common. Open year around. Daily: 3 game fish. Possession: 6 game fish. Hours parks are open to public use.

sources.title: Paradise Park Pond — Nevada Department of Wildlife
sources.quotedText: The Paradise Park Pond complex consists of two large ponds and two small ponds that accumulate about 24 surface acres. The larger ponds are about 18 feet deep and have numerous boulder structures that enhance fish habitat. These two ponds are the ones stocked with rainbow and brown trout, bluegill, and channel catfish. Accessible fishing piers are found at the north and south ponds. Stocking Updates (selected): 1001 Rainbow Trout, 9.2 in, 03-10-2026; 507 Rainbow Trout, 10.6 in, 09-18-2025; 500 Channel Catfish, 4-6 in, 05-29-2025; 1018 Rainbow Trout, 9.7 in, 03-06-2025.

sources.title: Apply & Buy — Nevada Fishing Licenses — NDOW
sources.quotedText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.

regulations.citation: NBWC CR 25-16 — Washoe County Community Fishing Ponds
regulations.humanSummary: Paradise Ponds are open to fishing year-round.

parameters.counting_rule: No species-specific sub-limit is stated for Paradise Ponds in NDOW's current regulation table; the limit applies to the combined daily/possession total across all game fish species.

regulations.citation: NBWC CR 25-16 — Washoe County Community Fishing Ponds (incl. Paradise Ponds)
regulations.humanSummary: Daily limit 3 game fish; possession limit 6 game fish (all species combined) at Paradise Ponds.
regulations.verbatimText: Daily: 3 game fish. Possession: 6 game fish.

parameters.note: NDOW's commission-regulation table lists hours for Washoe County Community Fishing Ponds generically as 'hours parks are open to public use'; no fixed clock times or sunrise/sunset offsets are specified for Paradise Ponds (within Teglia's Paradise Park, a City of Reno park).

regulations.citation: NBWC CR 25-16 — Washoe County Community Fishing Ponds
regulations.humanSummary: Fishing at Paradise Ponds is allowed during the hours the park is open to the public.
regulations.verbatimText: Hours parks are open to public use

parameters.note: Standard Nevada resident/nonresident fishing license required for anglers 12 and older; no water-specific permit exists for Paradise Ponds.

regulations.humanSummary: A Nevada fishing license is required for anglers age 12 and older at Paradise Ponds; no license is required under age 12.
regulations.verbatimText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.
